findings.reachability: Unauthenticated via wp_ajax_nopriv_ AJAX action (DGWT_WCAS_SEARCH_ACTION) and WooCommerce AJAX endpoint; any visitor can call it
findings.justification: The handler is registered with both wp_ajax_nopriv_ and WC AJAX (line 114) with no nonce, capability check, or rate-limit, allowing unauthenticated enumeration of product data including SKUs, prices, descriptions, and internal product IDs via the 'product-ids' context parameter.

detection_pattern.section_name: Missing Auth: wp_ajax_nopriv_ handler without access control
detection_pattern.description: Finds AJAX handlers exposed to unauthenticated users (wp_ajax_nopriv_). When these handlers return non-public sensitive data (SKUs, internal IDs, draft content) without a capability check, they represent information disclosure. Files matching wp_ajax_nopriv_ but not containing check_ajax_referer, wp_verify_nonce, or current_user_can nearby are suspect.

findings.reachability: Any page that can trigger an AJAX request to the search endpoint; exploitable by any page the logged-in user visits
findings.justification: The developer explicitly suppressed PHPCS's nonce warning (//phpcs:disable WordPress.Security.NonceVerification.Recommended) rather than adding verification; $_REQUEST['dgwt_wcas_keyword'] and $_REQUEST['s'] are read without any nonce check, so a cross-site request can force a logged-in user's browser to issue search queries and reveal returned data via CORS or redirect.

detection_pattern.section_name: CSRF: PHPCS nonce suppression without actual nonce verification
detection_pattern.description: Flags files where the developer disabled PHPCS nonce checks without adding a real nonce verification call. This pattern is a code smell that frequently corresponds to a CSRF vulnerability; it is more reliable than searching for the absence of a nonce.

findings.location: getCategories() line 535, getTags() line 587, getProductsData() line 388-392
findings.reachability: Any visitor triggering a search; the XSS fires in the browser when the JavaScript frontend renders results using innerHTML rather than textContent
findings.justification: Category/tag names are passed through html_entity_decode() (lines 535, 587) and product names through html_entity_decode() (line 390) before being JSON-encoded into the response; if the client-side JavaScript renders the 'value' field via innerHTML (a reasonable assumption for rich search dropdowns showing formatted product names), a stored payload in a product/category name — which an admin can set — would execute in every visitor's browser during search.

detection_pattern.section_name: XSS: html_entity_decode on user-controlled data without re-escaping
detection_pattern.description: html_entity_decode() reverses HTML entity encoding, so if the decoded string is placed into HTML output or into a JSON field that JavaScript renders as HTML, any HTML/JS in the original stored value becomes active. Files that call html_entity_decode but lack a subsequent esc_html/esc_attr/wp_kses before outputting are suspect for stored XSS.

findings.reachability: Unauthenticated via the nopriv AJAX endpoint by passing context=product-ids or product-ids-ff in the request
findings.justification: The 'product-ids' and 'product-ids-ff' context values (lines 263, 279) are read from the request ($context parameter flow) and cause the handler to return raw WP_Post objects or IDs for ALL matching products regardless of user role, potentially exposing hidden or not-yet-linked product IDs to unauthenticated callers who can enumerate the store's product catalog.

detection_pattern.section_name: Info Disclosure: Product ID enumeration via context parameter
detection_pattern.description: Flags endpoints that branch on a caller-supplied context value (e.g., 'product-ids') to return bulk product IDs without checking whether the caller is authorized to see that data. Absence of current_user_can or is_user_logged_in near these branches indicates unauthenticated bulk data access.

findings.reachability: Requires Polylang active without WooCommerce Polylang integration; the $request parameter comes from a WordPress filter chain meaning it may incorporate URL parameters from external sources
findings.justification: The method hooks woocommerce_ajax_get_endpoint and receives a $request string from the filter without sanitizing it before passing it to add_query_arg('wc-ajax', $request, $url); add_query_arg does not escape its values for HTML output contexts, so if the composed URL is later echoed without esc_url() in a theme or WooCommerce template, a crafted wc-ajax value could inject additional query parameters or cause a redirect.

detection_pattern.section_name: Open Redirect / URL Injection: add_query_arg without esc_url on output
detection_pattern.description: add_query_arg() does not HTML-encode its output; when its return value is echoed in HTML without esc_url(), an attacker who controls any input parameter flowing into it can inject arbitrary URL characters. Files that call add_query_arg but do not pass the result through esc_url() before output are suspect.
